text: Anaerobic capacity and muscle strength are greatest during the follicular phase of the menstrual cycle, when estrogen levels peak.
Resistance training conducted in the follicular phase of the menstrual cycle may produce superior gains in muscle strength and mass compared to luteal phase-based resistance training.
Fluctuations in estrogen and progesterone during the menstrual cycle regulate protein metabolism and recovery processes in skeletal muscle, potentially affecting training adaptation.
Female athletes may optimize resistance training outcomes by prioritizing higher-intensity or higher-volume resistance sessions during the follicular phase of their menstrual cycle.
